lesson_objective: A class is a blueprint that defines what an object will have and do; an object is one specific thing built from that blueprint.
lesson_summary: This lesson uses a cookie-cutter analogy to show the difference between defining a Car class once and creating three distinct Car objects from it, each with its own data but the same shape.
key_points: A class defines the shape (fields and behavior) that every object built from it will have.
An object is one specific instance — its own copy of that shape, filled in with its own data.
The same class can produce many objects, each independent of the others.
step_by_step_explanation: Open on the cookie-cutter analogy: one cutter, many cookies, each one separate.
Define a simple Car class with two fields: color and speed.
Create three Car objects from that one class, each with different color and speed values.
Change one Car object's speed and show the other two are completely unaffected.
Land on the takeaway: the class is the shape, the object is the thing — and they're not the same word for the same idea.

core_example.description: A minimal Car class instantiated three times with different field values, showing independence between instances.
core_example.focus_note: Highlight new Car() appearing twice — that's the moment two separate objects come from one class definition.

analogy: A class is like a cookie cutter, and an object is one cookie: the cutter defines the shape, but each cookie that comes out of it is its own separate cookie.
common_mistakes: Confusing the class definition with an instance of it — writing 'class Car' doesn't create a car.
Putting unrelated behavior into one class instead of modeling it around what the class actually represents.
what_to_avoid_saying: Do not say defining a class 'creates' an object — the class only becomes an object when new is used to instantiate it.
Do not say two objects from the same class 'share' their field values — each instance has its own independent copy.
beginner_takeaway: A class is the shape; an object is the thing built from it — and you can build more than one.
retention_hook: One cookie cutter. Three cookies. Same shape, three completely separate cookies — that's a class and its objects.
visual_notes: Show the cookie-cutter visual dissolve into the Car class definition as the analogy transitions into code.
Show three Car objects on screen simultaneously with their distinct color/speed values labeled.

flagged_uncertainties: Curriculum prerequisites list java-static-vs-instance alongside java-method-basics for this topic; this lesson assumes static-vs-instance has already been taught, per the topic selector's readiness gate, and does not re-explain it.
